description: LCMA (Lithos Cognitive Memory Architecture) turns Lithos from 'notes + embeddings' into a cognitive substrate. MVP 1 delivers the core retrieval infrastructure: a new lithos_retrieve MCP tool orchestrating seven parallel scouts against existing Lithos stores plus two new SQLite stores (edges.db, stats.db), producing ranked results compatible with lithos_search. Purely additive — no existing tool, format, test, or signature is modified.

userStories.title: LCMA frontmatter fields with lazy read-time defaults
userStories.description: As an agent reading a pre-LCMA note, I want LCMA fields to materialize with default values at read time so that notes authored before LCMA remain fully readable and no migration is required.
acceptanceCriteria: KnowledgeMetadata exposes optional LCMA fields: schema_version (int, default 1), namespace (str, derived from path), access_scope (shared|task|agent_private, default shared), note_type (observation|agent_finding|summary|concept|task_record|hypothesis, default observation), status (active|archived|quarantined, default active), summaries (optional nested object with short and long)
Reading any pre-LCMA note (no LCMA fields in frontmatter) returns a KnowledgeDocument with all LCMA defaults materialized in-memory

Defaults are applied at read time in KnowledgeManager.read() and the equivalent sync-from-disk path, NOT inside KnowledgeMetadata.from_dict. from_dict retains its current (data: dict) signature and only unpacks the keys it receives; namespace derivation and LCMA defaults are applied by the caller that has access to the note's relative path
Namespace derivation uses note's path relative to LithosConfig.storage.knowledge_path: subdirectory components joined by /, or 'default' if file is directly under knowledge_path
Explicit namespace in frontmatter overrides derivation
KnowledgeMetadata.to_dict round-trip is non-lossy; no existing test in test_knowledge.py is modified
Unit tests cover: each default for pre-LCMA notes, namespace derivation for each path convention, explicit namespace override, round-trip preservation, from_dict without path info works unchanged
Pyright, ruff, ruff format --check, and all Done Criteria pass

userStories.title: lithos_write accepts optional LCMA fields
userStories.description: As an agent creating or updating a note, I want to optionally pass LCMA metadata through lithos_write so I can author notes with explicit namespaces, note types, and summaries without breaking existing callers.
acceptanceCriteria: lithos_write gains optional keyword parameters: schema_version, namespace, access_scope, note_type, status, summaries — all defaulting to None; no existing parameter renamed/removed/reordered
The existing lithos_write status envelope shape (status, code, message, warnings, etc.) is unchanged; LCMA fields do not add new top-level status keys

Preserve/set semantics on update: schema_version/note_type/access_scope/status write defaults if absent, preserve if set; summaries left absent if not provided; namespace derived at read time only, persisted only if explicitly passed; entities not accepted in MVP 1 (deferred to MVP 2; lithos-enrich will populate)

access_scope='task' requires source_task on create (or existing metadata.source on update); violation returns error envelope {status: error, code: invalid_input, message: "access_scope='task' requires source_task"}

On update, source_task is forwarded to KnowledgeManager.update() so that metadata.source can be set or changed; if source_task is omitted on update, the existing metadata.source value is preserved (not cleared)
Invalid enum values return {status: error, code: invalid_input} without raising
Every pre-existing test that calls lithos_write without LCMA params passes unchanged

Unit tests cover: create with LCMA fields, update preserving existing values, update on pre-LCMA note writes defaults except namespace, invalid enum rejected, summaries persists in YAML, task-scope invariant enforcement
Pyright, ruff, ruff format --check, unit, and integration tests all pass

userStories.title: Create edges.db with edges table on first use
userStories.description: As the Lithos server, I want to lazily create data/.lithos/edges.db with its schema on first use so that typed edge storage is available without an explicit migration step.
acceptanceCriteria: On first call to any internal edge-store operation (e.g. the edges store open/get helper), edges.db is created if absent with edges table and indexes: edge_id PK, from_id, to_id, type, weight, namespace, created_at, updated_at, provenance_actor, provenance_type, evidence, conflict_state. US-010's lithos_edge_upsert and lithos_edge_list must call this same helper
UNIQUE(from_id, to_id, type, namespace) constraint; edge_id is auto-generated edge_<short-uuid>; upserts target composite unique key
Indexes on (from_id), (to_id), (type), (namespace)
Store location respects LithosConfig.storage.data_dir
The database handle is safe for concurrent reads and sequential writes from a single process (matches the existing coordination.db pattern)
Corrupt edges.db is quarantined (renamed to edges.db.corrupt-<ISO-timestamp>) and recreated with empty schema
Opening existing edges.db does not drop or recreate rows
Unit tests verify: DB + schema creation on first use, idempotent re-open, no-op when DB exists
Pyright, ruff, and all Done Criteria pass

userStories.title: Create stats.db with five MVP 1 tables on first use
userStories.description: As the Lithos server, I want to lazily create data/.lithos/stats.db with all MVP 1 tables so that retrieval can write receipts and working-memory rows from the very first query.
acceptanceCriteria: On first use, stats.db is created with tables: node_stats, coactivation, enrich_queue, working_memory, receipts — schemas matching design doc section 4.4
JSON array columns (scouts_fired, final_nodes, conflicts_surfaced) are TEXT NOT NULL containing JSON arrays; namespace_filter is TEXT NULL (SQL NULL when None, JSON array string when provided)
Idempotent open: existing stats.db preserves all rows
Store location respects LithosConfig.storage.data_dir
Corrupt stats.db is quarantined and recreated with empty schema (matching edges.db pattern)
Unit tests verify: schema creation, idempotent re-open, insert/select round-trip for each table including receipts.namespace_filter round-trip for both None and list, corrupt-file recovery
Pyright, ruff, all Done Criteria pass

userStories.title: LcmaConfig configuration subtree
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a typed LCMA configuration subtree so I can tune retrieval behaviour via env vars and config files without code changes.
acceptanceCriteria: LithosConfig gains optional lcma: LcmaConfig subtree with fields: enabled (bool, default True), enrich_drain_interval_minutes (int, default 5), rerank_weights (dict, MVP 1 defaults: {"vector": 0.35, "lexical": 0.25, "exact_alias": 0.15, "tags_recency": 0.10, "freshness": 0.05, "provenance": 0.05, "task_context": 0.05} — keys correspond one-to-one with the seven canonical scout names minus the scout_ prefix), note_type_priors (dict, required keys exactly the six note_type enum values: {"observation": 0.5, "agent_finding": 0.5, "summary": 0.5, "concept": 0.5, "task_record": 0.5, "hypothesis": 0.5}; all default 0.5; unknown keys rejected at validation; missing keys filled with 0.5 at load), temperature_default (float, default 0.5), temperature_edge_threshold (int, default 50), wm_eviction_days (int, default 7), llm_provider (str | None, default None)
Env var prefix follows LITHOS_LCMA__* convention
Adding LcmaConfig does not change any existing LithosConfig field name, type, default, or env var
Defaults allow MVP 1 to function without any user configuration
note_type_priors with missing keys are filled to all six entries with default 0.5; unknown keys are rejected at validation time

Unit tests cover: default construction, env var override, no side effects on unrelated config subtrees, enabled=False is loadable and queryable (runtime behaviour when enabled=False is covered in US-009), note_type_priors key filling and rejection
Pyright, ruff, all Done Criteria pass

userStories.title: edge.upserted event type and EventBus maxsize parameter
userStories.description: As a future lithos-enrich worker, I want an edge.upserted event in the bus and a way to request a larger subscriber queue so that MVP 2 can subscribe without schema changes.
acceptanceCriteria: events.py adds EDGE_UPSERTED = 'edge.upserted' as an event type constant
EventBus.subscribe() accepts optional maxsize: int | None = None parameter; if None, uses self._queue_size; if provided, subscriber queue uses that size
No existing subscribe() caller is modified
Every existing event bus test passes unchanged
Unit tests cover: edge.upserted publish/receive, subscribe(maxsize=10_000) returns larger queue, subscribe() with no argument unchanged
Pyright, ruff, all Done Criteria pass

userStories.title: Async wrapping of sync search calls at server call sites
userStories.description: As the Lithos server, I must not block the asyncio event loop when lithos_retrieve fires parallel scouts. Wrap synchronous search APIs with asyncio.to_thread() at every call site reused by scouts.
acceptanceCriteria: Every call site invoking SearchEngine.full_text_search, semantic_search, or hybrid_search from async code uses await asyncio.to_thread(...)
No change to synchronous signatures of SearchEngine methods
Existing lithos_search behaviour and tests are unchanged
New unit test verifies a long-running synchronous search does not block a concurrent lithos_search call
Pyright, ruff, all Done Criteria pass

userStories.title: Scout interface and the seven MVP 1 scouts
userStories.description: As the retrieval pipeline, I want seven scouts that wrap existing Lithos infrastructure, each returning Candidate objects with raw scores, so that merge_and_normalize can turn them into a normalized candidate pool.
acceptanceCriteria: A scout module under src/lithos/lcma/ (e.g. src/lithos/lcma/scouts.py) defines exactly seven async-safe scout functions: scout_vector (ChromaDB via asyncio.to_thread), scout_lexical (Tantivy via asyncio.to_thread), scout_exact_alias (KnowledgeGraph._resolve_link plus UUID-prefix and slug matching via KnowledgeManager.get_id_by_slug), scout_tags_recency (returns [] when both tags and path_prefix are absent; otherwise KnowledgeManager.list_all() with filters sorted by updated_at), scout_freshness (expires_at/is_stale boost, keyword-triggered on update/refresh/recheck/verify), scout_provenance (derived_from_ids forward/reverse walk via existing provenance index), scout_task_context (activated only when task_id provided; pulls notes from findings.knowledge_id ignoring NULL rows, plus notes whose metadata.author matches an agent with non-expired claims for that task_id). These names are the exact canonical values that appear in receipts.scouts_fired
scout_contradictions is a non-counted internal no-op stub returning []; never included in receipts.scouts_fired; conflicts_surfaced is always [] in MVP 1
Each scout returns list[Candidate] with raw scores; normalization is handled by merge_and_normalize
All seven scouts apply namespace_filter gating before returning candidates
access_scope gating: task-scoped notes excluded without matching task_id (via metadata.source); agent-private notes excluded without matching agent_id (via metadata.author)
Legacy tools (lithos_search, lithos_read, lithos_list) remain backward-compatible and do not gain caller-context access-scope gating in MVP 1

Unit tests cover each scout individually with seeded KnowledgeManager: raw score Candidate objects returned, namespace filter honoured, access_scope gating correct, scout_task_context returns [] when task_id is None, scout_tags_recency returns [] when both tags and path_prefix are absent, contradictions stub returns []
Pyright, ruff, all Done Criteria pass

userStories.title: lithos_retrieve MCP tool — orchestration, receipts, working memory
userStories.description: As an agent querying Lithos, I want a single MCP tool that runs all seven scouts, returns a ranked result compatible with lithos_search, and writes an audit receipt on every call.
acceptanceCriteria: lithos_retrieve registered with signature: query (required), limit=10, namespace_filter, agent_id, task_id, surface_conflicts, max_context_nodes, tags, path_prefix — all optional

Response is structural superset of lithos_search: results list with id, title, snippet, score (normalized [0,1]), path, source_url, updated_at, is_stale, derived_from_ids plus LCMA extras: reasons, scouts, salience; envelope adds temperature, terrace_reached, receipt_id. The values of snippet, source_url, is_stale, and derived_from_ids must be identical to those produced by lithos_search for the same note (shared utility or equivalent logic with integration-test parity assertion)
lithos_retrieve does not modify lithos_search behaviour or signature; clients continue to call lithos_search directly if they prefer; lithos_retrieve is purely additive
When max_context_nodes is omitted, it defaults to limit; this default is applied before any scout execution

Phase A scouts (vector, lexical, exact_alias, tags_recency, freshness, task_context when task_id provided) run in parallel via asyncio.gather; Phase B (provenance) runs sequentially seeded from top max_context_nodes of normalized Phase A pool
merge_and_normalize produces normalized candidate pool; Terrace 1 rerank_fast applies diversity (MMR), note_type priors, and basic salience
Temperature: cold-start returns LcmaConfig.temperature_default when edges.db has fewer than temperature_edge_threshold edges in dominant namespace
When LcmaConfig.enabled is False, returns {status: error, code: lcma_disabled, message: "LCMA is disabled via configuration"} without executing scouts or writing receipt

Every call writes a row to stats.db.receipts (id format rcpt_<short-uuid>), including calls that raise exceptions (receipt written in finally with terrace_reached=0 and partial scout data). When LcmaConfig.enabled is False, no receipt is written (error envelope returned immediately)
When task_id is provided, every returned result causes upsert into stats.db.working_memory keyed on (task_id, node_id): activation_count increments, last_seen_at updates, last_receipt_id set
Unit tests cover Phase A parallelism, Phase B sequencing, normalization, Terrace 1 reranking, cold-start temperature, enabled=False error, receipt row format, working memory upsert
Integration test: first call against fresh data dir creates edges.db and stats.db with exactly one receipt row
Integration test: ten calls produce ten receipt rows

Integration test: response passes compatibility assertion against lithos_search response schema — every key present in a lithos_search result is present in each lithos_retrieve result with the same type
Integration test: snippet, source_url, is_stale, and derived_from_ids values match lithos_search output for the same notes (semantic parity)
Integration test: lithos_retrieve with task_id writes working_memory rows and increments activation_count on second call
Integration test: receipts.scouts_fired contains all seven scout names when conditions met
Pyright, ruff, all Done Criteria pass

userStories.title: lithos_edge_upsert and lithos_edge_list MCP tools
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to create, update, and query typed edges in edges.db so that LCMA's graph layer is usable in MVP 1.
acceptanceCriteria: lithos_edge_upsert creates or updates a typed edge with params: from_id, to_id, type, weight, namespace (required), provenance_actor, provenance_type, evidence (dict|list|None only — scalars rejected), conflict_state; upsert key is (from_id, to_id, type, namespace); returns edge_id in status envelope
lithos_edge_list queries edges by from_id, to_id, type, namespace or combinations; returns {results: list[edge_dict]} with exactly the specified keys and types
lithos_edge_upsert publishes edge.upserted event via event bus
Neither tool modifies existing NetworkX wiki-link graph or lithos_links behaviour
Concurrent writes from a single process are safe (single-writer SQLite pattern matching coordination.db)
Missing namespace returns error envelope; scalar evidence returns error envelope
Unit tests cover: create, update-by-composite-key, query by each dimension, event emission, idempotent upsert
Pyright, ruff, all Done Criteria pass

userStories.title: Coactivation update on retrieval
userStories.description: As the retrieval pipeline, I want to record node co-occurrence in final result sets so that MVP 2's reinforcement can use MVP 1 coactivation data.
acceptanceCriteria: After writing the receipt, lithos_retrieve increments coactivation.count for every unordered pair in final_nodes within dominant namespace and updates last_at
Dominant namespace is most common namespace among final_nodes; ties broken alphabetically
node_stats.retrieval_count incremented and last_retrieved_at updated for every node in final_nodes; row inserted with salience 0.5 on first touch
Unit + integration tests cover single-call and multi-call invariants
Pyright, ruff, all Done Criteria pass

userStories.title: Internal provenance-to-edges projection
userStories.description: As the reconciler, I want a new internal function that projects derived_from_ids from frontmatter into edges.db while preserving the existing _reconcile_provenance_projection noop contract.
acceptanceCriteria: New internal function _project_provenance_to_edges (or similar) ensures edges.db has type='derived_from' edge for each derived_from_ids entry and removes orphan edges
Existing _reconcile_provenance_projection remains a noop and its existing tests in tests/test_reconcile.py pass unchanged
_project_provenance_to_edges is not exposed as MCP tool; is no-op when edges.db does not exist
No existing test in tests/test_reconcile.py is modified
Unit tests cover: forward projection, stale edge removal, idempotent repeat runs, no-op when edges.db absent
Pyright, ruff, all Done Criteria pass
